name: Hat Man Shadow
description: The Hat Man Shadow is a phenomenon reported globally, characterized by sightings of a shadowy figure, often male, wearing a wide-brimmed hat, and sometimes a coat or trench coat. Witnesses typically describe a feeling of unease, fear, or dread associated with the figure, which is often seen during periods of sleep paralysis or heightened stress. Proponents suggest the Hat Man is a supernatural entity, possibly a shadow person, interdimensional being, or even a manifestation of collective fear. Some theories link him to the occult or darker spiritual realms. The origins of the modern phenomenon are difficult to pinpoint, but online forums and paranormal communities have popularized the concept. Key evidence cited includes personal anecdotes, shared experiences on the internet, and purported photographic or video evidence, often of poor quality and open to interpretation. Mainstream perspectives generally attribute these sightings to hypnagogic hallucinations, sleep paralysis, misidentification of mundane objects in low light, and the power of suggestion fueled by online narratives and collective imagination. Skeptics emphasize the lack of verifiable evidence and the subjective nature of these experiences, attributing the similarities in descriptions to shared cultural tropes and psychological factors.
subtitle: Shadowy Figure in a Hat: A Global Phenomenon

claims.claim: The Hat Man is a sentient entity.
claims.evidence: Consistent descriptions across different cultures and time periods; feeling of malevolence or intent reported by witnesses.
claims.criticism: Shared cultural tropes and the power of suggestion can explain similar descriptions; perceived malevolence is likely a product of fear and sleep paralysis.

claims.claim: The Hat Man appears during sleep paralysis to deliver a message or warning.
claims.evidence: Many sightings occur during or immediately after episodes of sleep paralysis; some report receiving disturbing or prophetic messages.
claims.criticism: Sleep paralysis is known to induce vivid hallucinations and feelings of dread; any perceived messages are likely products of a disturbed mental state.

claims.claim: The Hat Man is an interdimensional being or shadow person.
claims.evidence: The figure is often described as being 'out of place' or not fully present in our reality; reports of flickering or disappearing.
claims.criticism: These descriptions are subjective and difficult to verify; can be attributed to pareidolia, optical illusions, or the effects of sleep deprivation.

keyFigures.name: Heidi Hollis
keyFigures.role: Author and researcher who popularized the Hat Man phenomenon

keyFigures.name: Brian Dunning
keyFigures.role: Science writer and skeptic who debunks paranormal claims, including Hat Man sightings

timeline.event: Emergence of Hat Man reports on online forums and paranormal websites.
Heidi Hollis publishes 'The Secret War', popularizing the Hat Man concept.
Continued discussion and sharing of Hat Man experiences online and in paranormal communities.

sources.author: J. Allan Hobson
sources.work: The Chemistry of Conscious States: How the Brain Changes Its Mind
sources.title: J. Allan Hobson

sources.author: Baland Jalal, V.S. Ramachandran
sources.work: Sleep Paralysis, ‘The Night Mare’, and Serial Killing: A Neurophenomenological Hypothesis
sources.title: Baland Jalal, V.S. Ramachandran
